mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for future equity cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE spikes >0.3pp, or trigger frequency drops below 5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales growth and deceleration in consumer price inflation indicates robust real demand without inflationary pressure, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS acceleration and CPIAUCSL deceleration yields negative 20-day SPX returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above 2% annualized during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth >2% during tightening periods correlates with negative 60-day SPX returns, or predictive power vanishes outside tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts decline exceeding 10% from the prior month signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and household balance sheet stress, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in >50% of months following a >10% HOUST drop, or trigger fails to predict direction with p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening that compresses wage growth expectations, supporting positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases combined with UNRATE decreases yield negative 5-day SPX returns in >65% of observations, or joint signal loses edge in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3% while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, real rates turn deeply negative, forcing portfolio rebalancing into equities and driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative in >55% of periods where CPIAUCSL > 3.0% and FEDFUNDS < 2.5%, or regime classification yields no alpha.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.5% annualized confirms macroeconomic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% correlates with negative 20-day SPX returns in >50% of quarters, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.
